title: The Pedagogical Engine: Learning Through Influence
overview: The dynamic teaching system of the Language Atlas, leveraging the Influence Graph to explain the "Why" of programming history.
historical_context: Created to solve the problem of static, linear documentation in the field of language history.
mental_model: A GPS for programming history, providing both curated "Odysseys" and dynamic "Auto-Odysseys".
key_aspects: Static Odysseys: Narrative arcs through a domain.
Dynamic Lineage: Recursive CTE-based influence tracing.
Challenge-Based Learning: Technical provocations linked to language profiles.
tradeoffs: Automated paths may lack the nuance of human curation, but they provide exhaustive coverage of the graph.
legacy: Defines the "Zenith" experience of the Language Atlas.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Explain how the influence_score is calculated across the database.
Analyze the SQL Recursive CTE used to generate dynamic Odysseys.
Design a new curated Odyssey using the existing influence graph.
